channel.title: Extraterrestrials on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Extraterrestrials on Alien Truth Social

item.title: Truman Bethurum
item.description:
Truman Bethurum was a well-known UFO contactee from the 1950s, famous for his 1954 book Aboard a Flying Saucer. He claimed to have been contacted by aliens from a utopian planet called Clarion, located on the other side of the Moon. Bethurum also founded the Sanctuary of Thought, a communal organization. While some believed him to be sincere, many UFO researchers dismissed his claims as implausible.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sanctuary_of_Thought

item.title: Christ Brotherhood
item.description:
The Christ Brotherhood, Inc. was a UFO religious group founded on December 16, 1956, in Orange, California, by Wallace C. Halsey. Halsey claimed to channel extraterrestrial messages warning of world destruction and the salvation of a remnant of humanity. The group began constructing a compound with underground bomb shelters in Cache Valley, Utah, in 1961, intended to house about 1000 people. The organization lost cohesion after Halsey disappeared in a plane crash in March 1963, though his teachings were later published posthumously.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christ_Brotherhood

item.title: Former President Obama Clarifies on Aliens During Podcast Interview
item.description:
Former President Barack Obama clarified his stance on extraterrestrials in an interview released on Saturday. While initially joking about aliens in a podcast clip, he later posted the clip to Instagram with a caption to provide further explanation. Obama stated that while statistically, life likely exists elsewhere in the vast universe, the chances of alien visitation are low. He emphasized that he saw no evidence of extraterrestrial contact during his presidency.

Source: https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/barack-obama-clarifies-seen-no-evidence-aliens-saying-real-rcna259232
